mcq.question: What is an array in C?
options: A collection of different data types
A collection of elements of the same data type

mcq.question: Why are arrays used?
options: To store multiple values of the same type

What is the index of the first element in an array?
How are array elements stored in memory?

How many elements can be stored in int arr[10]?

What is a two-dimensional array?
Which notation accesses the third element of arr?
What happens if an array index exceeds its size?
Which of the following is a multidimensional array?
What is array traversal?
Which operation is easier with arrays?
Arrays can store?
options: Elements of the same data type

mcq.question: What is the size of an array determined by?
Which data structure stores elements in contiguous memory locations?
Arrays are best suited for?
Why are arrays important?

true_false.statement: Array elements are stored in contiguous memory locations.
The first index of an array is 1.
Arrays store elements of the same data type.
A two-dimensional array has rows and columns.
Array indices start from 0 in C.
Arrays can grow dynamically by default.
Accessing an invalid index leads to undefined behavior.
Arrays are useful for storing multiple values.
A one-dimensional array uses a single index.
Array traversal means accessing all elements one by one.
